title: Cadence project adapter (cadence.config.json)
description: The SINGLE per-project customization point. Declares this project's gates, relevance rules, retrieval tool, and verify channels. Stack-neutral: the orchestrator never changes — only this file does.

gates.description: id -> failable check. The orchestrator runs these via run-gate.mjs and ingests only the signal.

failPattern.description: regex; if it matches output the gate fails (in addition to exit code)

successPattern.description: regex; output MUST match or the gate fails

relevance.description: OPT-IN. Run only the gates a diff can affect. Worth it only when the full suite is slow.

retrieval.description: Retrieval-first: prefer compressed queries over whole-file reads. Probed in order.

context.description: Context-window budget. By DEFAULT every subagent's total work must fit in the first 30% of its window (the high-attention region; avoids lost-in-the-middle / context rot). Enforced by capping inputs + scoping/decomposing tasks; briefed to the agent for self-monitoring.

subagentWindowFraction.description: fraction of the window all subagent work must fit within

inputReserveFraction.description: of that budget, the max share the INPUT (prompt + handed-in data) may occupy, leaving headroom for the agent's own reads/reasoning/output

models.description: model name -> context window in tokens; `default` is the fallback

onExceed.description: what to do when an input exceeds its cap

verifyChannels.description: Reliability-ranked behavioral checks (run highest-confidence first, auto-fallback). Stack presets: web (build+http), cli (run+assert stdout), library (test subset), service (start+health).

parallel.description: OPT-IN heavy part. Declared-disjoint worktrees for parallel implementation.

setup.description: per-worktree bootstrap, e.g. python -m venv .venv && .venv/bin/pip install -e .
